hindiTranslation: संकल्पसे उत्पन्न होनेवाली सम्पूर्ण कामनाओंका सर्वथा त्याग करके और मनसे ही इन्द्रियसमूहको सभी ओरसे हटाकर ॥
englishTranslation: Abandon every desire born of resolve, and restrain the whole field of the senses on all sides with the mind alone.
essence: Desire loses power when the mind stops feeding it.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Let go of every desire that rises from planning and craving. Use the mind to pull the senses back from every direction.
simpleInsight: Freedom starts when wanting stops dragging the senses outward.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is being taught how to steady the mind. Krishna now gives the practical method: drop desires born from thought, and gather the senses back before they scatter the inner life.
modernRelevance: You open your phone to check one thing, and twenty minutes vanish. The mind has to pull the senses back before they pull you apart.
translationLiteral: सङ्कल्पप्रभवान् (born from resolve) / कामान् (desires) / त्यक्त्वा (having abandoned) / सर्वान् (all) / अशेषतः (completely) / मनसा (by the mind) / एव (alone) / इन्द्रियग्रामम् (the group of senses) / विनियम्य (restraining) / समन्ततः (on all sides)
insight: It shifts attention from chasing impulses to actively gathering the senses under one steady mind.
